name: Rosettes
recipeIngredient: powdered sugar for sprinkling over top
recipeInstructions:
1. Add the sugar to the lightly beaten eggs.
2. Pour in the milk and beat until the mixture is smooth.
3. Gradually add the flour and salt, stirring continuously until fully incorporated.
4. Stir in the lemon extract.
5. Heat enough vegetable oil in a pan for deep frying (about 2 inches deep).
6. Place paper towels nearby to drain excess oil from the fried cookies.
6. Dip the rosette iron about three-quarters of the way into the batter, ensuring it is evenly coated but not overflowing.
7. Quickly lower the dipped iron into the hot oil and fry for 2-3 minutes or until the bubbles stop rising to the surface.
8. Remove the cookie from the oil with a slotted spoon and allow it to drain on the paper towels.
9. If the cookie sticks, gently tap it with the handle of a wooden spoon or use a fork to loosen any residue.
10. Once both the batter and the temperature are correct (the cookie should almost fall off when lifted), remove from the iron.
11. Sprinkle with powdered sugar and store in an airtight container. These freeze well for up to ten months.
